questions.a: The same subproblems appear repeatedly in a recursive solution.

questions.q: Why do overlapping subproblems make DP effective?
questions.a: You can cache answers once and reuse them instead of recomputing the same work.

questions.q: When does a problem not benefit from DP?
questions.a: When each subproblem is solved once already, as in plain divide-and-conquer.
An optimal solution can be built from optimal solutions to smaller subproblems.

questions.q: Why is optimal substructure required for DP?
questions.a: If subproblem optima do not compose correctly, the table will not lead to a correct global optimum.

questions.q: Why is longest simple path in a general graph a bad DP candidate?
questions.a: Because a locally optimal subpath does not necessarily extend to a globally optimal simple path.
Top-down DP with caching of recursive results.

questions.q: Why do people like memoization for first-pass DP solutions?
questions.a: It is often closest to the problem statement and easy to add to an existing recursive solution.

questions.q: When might memoization be less ideal than tabulation?
questions.a: When recursion depth is large or you want tighter control over iteration order and memory.
Bottom-up DP that fills a table iteratively.

questions.q: Why can tabulation be faster than memoization?
questions.a: It avoids recursion overhead and often accesses memory in a more predictable order.

questions.q: When is tabulation the better implementation choice?
questions.a: When you know a safe fill order and want better performance or a bounded stack footprint.

questions.q: What kind of state does knapsack-style DP usually use?
questions.a: Item index plus remaining capacity or target amount.

questions.q: Why does knapsack fit DP so well?
questions.a: Each choice splits into smaller versions of the same problem with reduced capacity.

questions.q: When should you expect a 1D or 2D DP table for a problem?
questions.a: When the answer depends on a sequence position plus another constraint like weight, sum, or budget.

questions.q: What do sequence DP problems like LCS or edit distance track?
questions.a: Prefixes or intervals of one or two sequences.

questions.q: Why are LCS and edit distance usually 2D DPs?
questions.a: Each state depends on the next smaller prefix of one or both strings.

questions.q: When can a sequence DP be space-optimized?
questions.a: When each row only depends on the previous row or a small sliding window of rows.
